paper.title: Is the RSGC4 (Alicante 8) cluster a real star cluster?: Peculiar radial velocities of red supergiant stars

extraction.summary: The paper discusses RSG candidates in RSGC4 and examines whether they are runaway or walkaway stars from the cluster. It concludes they are neither runaway nor walkaway stars. The paper never presents any object as possibly unbound from the Milky Way / Galactic potential.

candidate_groups_considered.description: RSG candidates in six RSGCs with radial velocities and proper motions from Gaia DR3, including 12 candidates in RSGC4.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper presents these objects as RSG candidates in RSGC4 and explicitly investigates whether they are runaway or walkaway stars from the cluster. It concludes they are neither runaway nor walkaway stars from the cluster. The paper never presents any object as possibly unbound from the Milky Way / Galactic potential.

source_refs.context: Subsection 'Unbounded stars in the cluster' investigates runaway/walkaway status and concludes the RSG candidates in RSGC4 are neither runaway nor walkaway stars from the cluster.
Summary and conclusion restating that the RSG candidates in RSGC4 are neither runaway nor walkway stars influenced by supernovae events.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Photometry and color information of RSG candidates in six RSGCs, including infrared excess diagnostics for RSGC4 candidates.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: This table provides photometric properties used to assess AGB contamination and IR excess in RSGC4 candidates. It does not present any object as a Galactic-unbound or HVS candidate.

source_refs.context: Table photo caption: photometry and color information of RSG candidates in six RSGCs. No mention of Galactic escape or unbound status.
